channel.title: Data Breach & Ransomware Aggregator
channel.description: Real-time data breach notifications from state registries, federal sources, and ransomware trackers

item.title: Evolve Mortgage Services on behalf of financial institutions
item.description:
Evolve Mortgage Services on behalf of financial institutions reported a state registry affecting 13501 records in Prosper, TX.
📊 Records Affected: 13501 | 📍 Location: Prosper, TX | 📰 Source: Maine AG | 🏷️ Type: State Registry

item.title: Iran Hacktivists Make Noise but Have Little Impact on War
item.description:
Iran-aligned groups are trying to make their mark in the Gulf, but the results have fallen short of remarkable.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Ex-NSA Directors Discuss 'Red Line' for Offensive Cyberattacks
item.description:
Four former NSA chiefs representing a near-complete history of US Cyber Command debate the role of offensive cyber in the government at RSAC.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: AI-Native Security Is a Must to Counter AI-Based Attacks
item.description:
Attacks by artificial intelligence agents are a reality. Experts at Nvidia's GTC conference say defenders need to use the same tools to fight them off.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Why a 'Near-Miss' Database Is Key to Improving Information Sharing
item.description:
Organizations disclose attack details, though information may be limited. What if they did the same with close calls?
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: SANS: Top 5 Most Dangerous New Attack Techniques to Watch
item.description:
For the first time, SANS Institute's five top attack techniques all have one thing in common — AI.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Blame Game: Why Public Cyber Attribution Carries Risks
item.description:
Publicly accusing an entity of a cyberattack could have negative consequences that organizations should consider before taking the plunge.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: AI-Powered Dependency Decisions Introduce, Ignore Security Bugs
item.description:
AI models often hallucinate or make costly mistakes when tasked with recommending software versions, upgrade paths, and security fixes — leading to significant technical debt.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: How Organizations Can Use Mistakes to Level Up Their Security Programs
item.description:
Organizations repeatedly expose ports, reuse passwords, and skip patches, creating security gaps that attackers exploit for breaches. An industry veteran outlines ways to fix these common mistakes.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Critical Flaw in Langflow AI Platform Under Attack
item.description:
Threats actors pounced on the code injection vulnerability within hours of its disclosure, demonstrating that organizations have little time to address critical bugs.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Automotive Cybersecurity Threats Grow in Era of Connected, Autonomous Vehicles
item.description:
More than a decade since the 2015 Jeep hack, the cybersecurity of vehicles remains of the utmost importance.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Coruna, DarkSword & Democratizing Nation-State Exploit Kits
item.description:
Nation-state malware is being sold on the Dark Web and leaked to GitHub; and ordinary organizations might not stand much of a chance of defending themselves.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Infrastructure Attacks With Physical Consequences Down 25%
item.description:
Operational technology (OT) at industrial and critical infrastructure sites seem to have been benefitting from a lull in ransomware, and hackers' relative ignorance of OT systems.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Wartime Usage of Compromised IP Cameras Highlight Their Danger
item.description:
The list of countries exploiting Internet-connected cameras to give them eyes inside their adversaries' borders continues to expand. What should companies look out for?
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Chinese Hackers Caught Deep Within Telecom Backbone Infrastructure
item.description:
The state-sponsored threat actor deployed kernel implants and passive backdoors enabling long-term, high-level espionage. The post Chinese Hackers Caught Deep Within Telecom Backbone Infrastructure appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Hightower Holding Data Breach Impacts 130,000
item.description:
The holdings company says hackers stole names, Social Security numbers, and driver’s license numbers from its environment. The post Hightower Holding Data Breach Impacts 130,000 appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: CISA Flags Critical PTC Vulnerability That Had German Police Mobilized
item.description:
Police in Germany physically warned organizations about the critical PTC Windchill vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-4681. The post CISA Flags Critical PTC Vulnerability That Had German Police Mobilized appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Coruna iOS Exploit Kit Likely an Update to Operation Triangulation
item.description:
Coruna contains the updated version of a kernel exploit used in Operation Triangulation three years ago. The post Coruna iOS Exploit Kit Likely an Update to Operation Triangulation appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Pro-Iranian Hacking Group Claims Credit for Hack of FBI Director Kash Patel’s Personal Account
item.description:
The group that it was making available for download emails and other documents from Patel’s account. The post Pro-Iranian Hacking Group Claims Credit for Hack of FBI Director Kash Patel’s Personal Account appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Dutch court threatens xAI with fines over Grok's nonconsensual nude images
item.description:
Grok — which is owned by Elon Musk’s xAI — will be forced to pay damages of €100,000 ($115,000) a day if it does not comply, according to the ruling.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: FBI confirms theft of director’s personal emails by Iran-linked hacking group
item.description:
An FBI spokesperson told Recorded Future News that the information is “historical in nature and involves no government information,” adding that the agency has “taken all necessary steps to mitigate potential risks associated with this activity."
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
item.description:
Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
item.description:
AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and n
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
item.description:
A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
item.description:
The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
item.description:
A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.description:
Verification alert Listings attributed to QILIN have been reported as including unverified or fabricated victim...
🎭 Threat Actor: QILIN | 📰 Source: Red Packet Security | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

item.title: Telehealth Platform Provider OpenLoop Health Disclosed Data Breach
item.description:
A major data breach has been reported by the telehealth platform provider OpenLoop Health Inc. While the total number of […] The post Telehealth Platform Provider OpenLoop Health Disclosed Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Florida Insurance Commissioner Suspends Mirra Health for Medicare Data Transfers to Foreign Companies
item.description:
The sensitive data of more than 23,000 Florida Medicare members has been impermissibly shared with overseas companies, putting Medicare members’ […] The post Florida Insurance Commissioner Suspends Mirra Health for Medicare Data Transfers to Foreign Companies appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Deaconess Health System Affected by Vendor Data Breach
item.description:
Evansville, Indiana-based Deaconess Health System has announced a data breach involving information shared with a third-party vendor, the MRO Corp-owned […] The post Deaconess Health System Affected by Vendor Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: BakerHostetler: Healthcare Remains Most Targeted Sector with Extortion-Only Attacks on the Rise
item.description:
Healthcare has retained its position as the industry most targeted by cyber actors, an unwanted accolade that the sector has […] The post BakerHostetler: Healthcare Remains Most Targeted Sector with Extortion-Only Attacks on the Rise appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Excelsior Orthopaedics; Buffalo Surgery Center Pay $2.4 Million to Settle Data Breach Lawsuit
item.description:
A settlement has been reached to resolve class action data breach litigation against Excelsior Orthopaedics and Buffalo Surgery Center. The […] The post Excelsior Orthopaedics; Buffalo Surgery Center Pay $2.4 Million to Settle Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Data Breaches Reported by New York & Texas Plastic Surgery Practices
item.description:
Data breaches have recently been reported by Vantage Plastic Surgery in New York City and Austin Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery […] The post Data Breaches Reported by New York & Texas Plastic Surgery Practices appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Six New Healthcare Data Breaches Announced
item.description:
Data breaches have been announced by New Horizons Behavioral Health in Georgia, CWA Local 1180 in New York, Coastal Carolina […] The post Six New Healthcare Data Breaches Announced appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Anime streaming giant Crunchyroll says hacker stole data related to customer service tickets
item.description:
Jonathan Greig reports: The popular anime streaming platform Crunchyroll confirmed on Monday evening that a batch of customer information leaked online over the weekend is legitimate. In a statement to Recorded Future News, a spokesperson for the company said their investigation into the stolen documents is ongoing alongside cybersecurity experts. “At this time, we believe... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Malware on Luxembourg public sector devices was active for almost a month
item.description:
Alex Stevensson reports: Thousands of devices owned by the Luxembourg public sector found to be infected with malware at the end of February have since been updated and secured, digitalisation minister Stéphanie Obertin has said. The security breach was confirmed on 27 February but details were scant at the time, with LSAP deputy Ben Polidori... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Utimaco Survey Finds 78% of US Companies Name Legacy Data Breaches the Top Gen AI Risk
item.description:
From a press release by Utimaco: 57% of respondents have not yet implemented a solution to address these concerns Post quantum cryptography (PQC) readiness shows an even wider gap: while 72% identify legacy data attacks as the greatest quantum issue, 75% remain unprepared Data Sovereignty is front of mind: 80% of respondents rank protecting customer... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Companies face difficult choices in blaming hackers for an attack
item.description:
Eric Geller reports: Businesses need to think carefully about when they publicly blame a threat actor for a cyberattack, lest they invite unwanted consequences, experts said at a panel at the RSAC 2026 Conference here on Tuesday. “The rush to attribute is a risky one,” Megan Stifel, the chief strategy officer at the Institute for... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: TeamPCP Hits Trivy, Checkmarx, and LiteLLM in Credential Theft Campaign
item.description:
Deeba Ahmed reports on some of TeamPCP’s dangerously effective recent activities: What Happened? The trouble began on 19 March 2026, when a hacking group calling themselves TeamPCP managed to break into Trivy, a popular tool used by developers to scan their code for security vulnerabilities. This was a supply chain attack, which occurs when hackers sneak malicious code... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Delaware Supreme Court Reverses, Holds Cyber Insurers Sufficiently Pled Collective Subrogation Claim Resulting from Blackbaud Data Breach
item.description:
The fallout from the massive Blackbaud breach is not over, it seems. Lydia Mills of Wiley Rein writes: Reversing the decision below, the Delaware Supreme Court held that a group of cyber liability insurers sufficiently pled a complaint for subrogation based on breach of contract. Travelers Cas. & Sur. Co. of Am. v. Blackbaud, Inc., 2026... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Florida senator sues Booz Allen over his leaked tax returns
item.description:
Gary Fineout reports: Sen. Rick Scott is suing a major government contractor for damages after his tax returns were leaked along with other prominent and wealthy figures, including President Donald Trump. The Florida Republican on Monday filed a lawsuit against Booz Allen Hamilton, a management and technology consulting company, and a former employee of the contractor who... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran. But why?
item.description:
Brian Krebs reports: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran’s time zone or have Farsi set as the default language. Experts say the wiper campaign against... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Ransomware Attack Totally Cripples Jackson County Sheriff’s Office in Indiana
item.description:
From a report on cyber.netsecops.io: Executive Summary A debilitating ransomware attack has completely crippled the IT operations of the Jackson County Sheriff’s Office in Indiana. The attack, which struck last week, has rendered the department’s entire computer network, including all PCs, Wi-Fi, and critical reporting systems, unusable. […] Technical Analysis Initial Access Vector: The likely initial access vector... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: LeakBase’s “Chucky” detained in Russia
item.description:
TASS reports: Police have detained a Taganrog resident suspected of administering LeakBase, one of the largest hacker platforms. Law enforcement officials told TASS. The detained Taganrog resident is suspected of administering “one of the largest international hacker platforms, LeakBase,” the agency’s source said. According to him, the liquidated platform operated a credit system and user... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Russia arrests suspected owner of LeakBase cybercrime forum
item.description:
Russian police arrested a Taganrog resident believed to be the owner of LeakBase, a major online forum used by cybercriminals to buy and sell stolen data and hacking tools. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Coruna iOS exploit framework linked to Triangulation attacks
item.description:
The Coruna exploit kit is an evolution of the framework used in the Operation Triangulation espionage campaign, which in 2023 targeted iPhones via zero-click iMessage exploits. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Inside a Modern Fraud Attack: From Bot Signups to Account Takeovers
item.description:
Multi-stage fraud attacks chain bots, proxies, and stolen credentials from signup to takeover. IPQS shows why correlating IP, device, identity, and behavior is critical to stop it. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Ajax football club hack exposed fan data, enabled ticket hijack
item.description:
Dutch professional football club Ajax Amsterdam (AFC Ajax) disclosed that a hacker exploited vulnerabilities in its IT systems and accessed data belonging to a few hundred people. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Dutch Police discloses security breach after phishing attack
item.description:
The Dutch National Police (Politie) says a security breach resulting from a successful phishing attack has had a limited impact and hasn't affected citizens' data. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: European Commission investigating breach after Amazon cloud account hack
item.description:
The European Commission, the European Union's main executive body, is investigating a security breach after a threat actor gained access to the Commission's Amazon cloud environment. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Goodwill Industries
item.description:
Goodwill Industries reported a ransomware. Attributed to Interlock.
🎭 Threat Actor: Interlock | 📰 Source: BreachSense | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

item.title: American Association of Colleges of Osteopathic Medicine
